title: US Intelligence Reports Iran Accelerating Nuclear Program
description: US intelligence agencies release a declassified assessment indicating Iran has dramatically accelerated its nuclear enrichment program, reaching 87% purity at the Fordow facility. The report warns that Iran could assemble a crude nuclear device within weeks if enrichment continues at the current pace.
factCheckNote: Based on declassified ODNI assessment corroborated by IAEA monitoring data and satellite imagery from commercial providers

title: Emergency UN Security Council Session on Iran
description: The UN Security Council convenes an emergency session to discuss the Iranian nuclear crisis. The US pushes for authorization of military force, but faces opposition from Russia and China who call for continued diplomacy. The session ends without a resolution after a double veto.
factCheckNote: UN proceedings are publicly documented and broadcast; multiple news agencies present in chamber

title: Trump Authorizes Military Options Against Iran
description: President Trump signs a classified executive order authorizing the use of military force against Iranian nuclear and military infrastructure. Senior administration officials brief congressional leaders in a closed-door session. The Pentagon begins final positioning of forces in the region.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by White House press secretary and corroborated by multiple congressional sources who attended classified briefing

narratives.us: A necessary step to prevent a nuclear-armed Iran that threatens regional and global security
narratives.iran: An illegal act of aggression planned without UN authorization, violating international law and Iranian sovereignty

title: Iran's Khamenei Warns of 'Devastating Response' to Any Attack
description: Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei delivers a televised address warning that any military strike on Iran will be met with a 'devastating and unprecedented response.' He declares that Iran's military forces are fully prepared and that the Islamic Republic will defend its sovereignty at all costs.
factCheckNote: Broadcast live on Iranian state television (IRIB) and translated by multiple international news agencies

narratives.us: Threatening rhetoric from a regime that has destabilized the region for decades and is now on the verge of acquiring nuclear weapons
narratives.iran: A sovereign nation exercising its right to self-defense in the face of openly declared military aggression by the United States and Israel

title: US Launches Operation Epic Fury — Massive Aerial Campaign on Iran
description: The United States initiates Operation Epic Fury with a coordinated wave of over 200 cruise missiles launched from warships in the Arabian Sea and B-2 stealth bombers flying from Diego Garcia. Targets include nuclear facilities, air defense networks, IRGC headquarters, and command-and-control nodes across Iran.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Pentagon press briefing, satellite imagery from Planet Labs, and multiple independent journalists reporting from the region

narratives.us: A precise, targeted operation to eliminate Iran's nuclear weapons capability and protect American national security interests
narratives.iran: An unprovoked act of war and naked aggression against a sovereign nation, constituting a grave violation of the UN Charter

title: Israeli F-35s Join Strikes on Iranian Nuclear Facilities
description: The Israeli Air Force deploys F-35I Adir stealth fighters in a coordinated strike with US forces, targeting Iran's key nuclear facilities at Natanz, Fordow, and Isfahan. Israeli aircraft fly through Saudi airspace with tacit approval from Riyadh, marking an unprecedented level of regional cooperation against Iran.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by IDF spokesperson and corroborated by flight tracking data and Saudi silence on airspace violations

narratives.us: Israel exercising its right to self-defense by eliminating an existential nuclear threat on its doorstep
narratives.iran: The Zionist regime is a co-conspirator in American aggression, and both will face the full wrath of the Islamic Republic

title: Natanz Nuclear Facility Struck by Bunker Busters
description: Multiple GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators strike Iran's underground Natanz nuclear enrichment facility. The 30,000-pound bombs are designed to penetrate hardened underground structures. Satellite imagery later confirms significant damage to the facility's centrifuge halls located deep underground.
factCheckNote: Satellite imagery from multiple commercial providers confirms structural damage; seismic activity consistent with large-scale detonations detected

title: Isfahan Uranium Enrichment Site Hit
description: Coalition strikes hit the Isfahan uranium conversion facility, one of Iran's largest nuclear processing centers. Secondary explosions are reported, suggesting stockpiled materials were struck. Iranian state media initially denies any damage to nuclear facilities before going silent.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by satellite imagery and geolocated social media videos from Isfahan residents showing large explosions

title: Iran's Air Defense Network Targeted — Multiple S-300 Sites Destroyed
description: US Navy EA-18G Growler electronic warfare aircraft suppress Iranian air defenses while precision strikes destroy at least eight S-300 surface-to-air missile batteries. Iran's integrated air defense system is significantly degraded, allowing subsequent waves of strikes to proceed with reduced risk.
factCheckNote: Pentagon released battle damage assessment imagery; independent defense analysts confirm destruction of known S-300 positions

title: Khamenei Reported Killed in Leadership Decapitation Strike
description: Unconfirmed reports emerge that Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in a precision strike on a secure compound near Tehran. Iranian state media goes dark for several hours. US officials decline to confirm but note that 'senior leadership targets' were part of the operation.
factCheckNote: Multiple US and Israeli intelligence sources indicate strike hit intended target; Iranian state media silence for 6+ hours is unusual but death not independently confirmed

narratives.us: Decapitation of enemy leadership is a legitimate military objective that will hasten the end of hostilities and regime change
narratives.iran: The assassination of a nation's spiritual leader is a war crime that will only strengthen the resolve of the Iranian people to resist

title: Trump Addresses Nation: 'Iran Will Never Possess Nuclear Weapons'
description: President Trump delivers an address from the Oval Office announcing the commencement of military operations against Iran. He states the strikes are necessary to prevent Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons and frames the operation as defensive. He promises the campaign will be swift and decisive.
factCheckNote: Live broadcast carried by all major US networks and streamed globally; full transcript released by White House

narratives.us: The President acted decisively to protect American security and prevent a nuclear-armed rogue state from threatening the world
narratives.iran: The American president has launched an illegal war of aggression for political gain, repeating the lies used to justify the invasion of Iraq

title: IRGC Scrambles Forces, Declares 'Massive Retaliation' Imminent
description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issues a statement through IRNA declaring that Iran will launch a 'massive and crushing retaliation' against American and Israeli targets across the region. IRGC missile units are observed preparing mobile launchers at multiple sites throughout western Iran.
factCheckNote: IRGC statement broadcast on Iranian state media; satellite imagery from commercial providers confirms missile launcher mobilization

narratives.us: The IRGC's threats confirm why military action was necessary — Iran has always intended to use its military capability aggressively
narratives.iran: Iran is exercising its legitimate right to self-defense under Article 51 of the UN Charter after being subjected to an unprovoked attack

title: UN Secretary General Calls for Immediate Ceasefire
description: UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres issues an urgent appeal for an immediate ceasefire, warning that the conflict risks spiraling into a broader regional war. He calls on all parties to return to the negotiating table and urges Security Council members to find common ground.
factCheckNote: Official UN press statement and live press conference carried by international media

title: Oil Prices Surge 18% on War Fears
description: Brent crude surges 18% to $112 per barrel as markets react to the outbreak of war between the US and Iran. Fears of disruption to the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world's oil passes daily, drive panic buying. Energy traders warn prices could climb further if the strait is threatened.
factCheckNote: Market data from Bloomberg, Reuters, and major commodity exchanges confirm price movements

title: Iran Fires First Volley of Ballistic Missiles at Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar
description: Iran launches approximately 30 Shahab-3 and Qiam-1 ballistic missiles at Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar, the largest US air base in the Middle East and the forward headquarters of CENTCOM. The attack represents Iran's first major retaliatory strike and marks the first direct ballistic missile attack on a US base in the region.
factCheckNote: Launch detected by US early warning satellites; missile trajectories tracked by CENTCOM; impact confirmed by base personnel and satellite imagery

narratives.us: Iran's reckless attack on a military installation in a neutral country endangers the entire region and will be met with a forceful response
narratives.iran: The strike on Al Udeid is a proportional response targeting the very base from which America launched its cowardly aggression against Iran

title: US THAAD and Patriot Systems Intercept Majority of Incoming Missiles
description: The US military's layered missile defense system, including THAAD and Patriot PAC-3 batteries, successfully intercepts the majority of Iranian missiles targeting Al Udeid. The Pentagon reports a 90% interception rate, though several missiles penetrate the defense shield and impact the base perimeter.
factCheckNote: Pentagon provided interception data; independent defense analysts confirm based on debris patterns and satellite imagery analysis

title: Pentagon Confirms 'Minimal Damage' to Al Udeid
description: Pentagon spokesperson confirms that Al Udeid Air Base sustained 'minimal damage' from the Iranian missile attack, with no US casualties reported. Several non-critical facilities were damaged and one runway temporarily disabled. Flight operations resume within hours.
factCheckNote: Pentagon assessment released; however, independent journalists have limited access to base and full damage extent cannot be independently confirmed

narratives.us: America's advanced missile defenses proved their worth, protecting our forces and demonstrating technological superiority
narratives.iran: The Pentagon is concealing the true extent of damage; our missiles struck critical targets and caused far more destruction than the Americans admit

title: Iran Fires Fattah-2 Hypersonic Missiles Toward Israeli Targets
description: Iran launches its advanced Fattah-2 hypersonic ballistic missiles toward targets in Israel for the first time in combat. The missiles, capable of speeds exceeding Mach 13, are designed to evade Israeli missile defense systems. Israel's Arrow-3 system is tested against a real hypersonic threat for the first time.
factCheckNote: Launch confirmed by US satellite detection; Iranian state media claims Fattah-2 deployment but independent verification of missile type is ongoing

narratives.us: Iran's use of advanced hypersonic weapons against civilian population centers is a dangerous escalation that must be met with overwhelming force
narratives.iran: The Fattah-2 demonstrates that Iran possesses unstoppable weapons technology that no defense system can counter

title: Iranian Missiles Strike Port Shuaiba, Kuwait — 6 US Service Members KIA
description: A salvo of Iranian ballistic missiles strikes the Port Shuaiba logistics facility in Kuwait, killing six US service members and wounding over a dozen. The attack hits a fuel storage area and a barracks. These are the first confirmed American combat fatalities of the conflict.
factCheckNote: Pentagon confirms casualties by name after next-of-kin notification; Kuwaiti officials corroborate the strike and damage

narratives.us: Iran has killed American service members in a neutral country — this demands the strongest possible military response
narratives.iran: American soldiers stationed illegally across the region to wage war on Iran are legitimate military targets under international law

title: Israeli Iron Dome Intercepts Most Missiles — Beit Shemesh Suburb Hit
description: Israel's multi-layered air defense system intercepts the majority of incoming Iranian missiles, but at least two penetrate the shield and strike a residential area in Beit Shemesh, west of Jerusalem. Three Israeli civilians are killed and 14 wounded. Rescue operations begin immediately.
factCheckNote: Israeli emergency services confirm casualties; geolocated video and photographs from impact site verified by multiple outlets

narratives.us: Iran deliberately targeted Israeli civilian areas — this is terrorism, not legitimate military action
narratives.iran: The strike targeted military infrastructure near Beit Shemesh; any civilian casualties are the result of Israeli military installations being placed among population centers

title: SecDef Pete Hegseth: 'We Will Not Be Deterred'
description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth delivers a Pentagon press briefing confirming six US service members were killed in the Kuwait strike. He pledges that the United States will intensify operations and that Iran will pay a 'severe price' for every American life lost. He outlines additional force deployments to the region.
factCheckNote: Live Pentagon press briefing carried by all major news networks; official DoD transcript released

title: Iran's Acting Supreme Leader Vows Continued Retaliation
description: With Khamenei's fate still officially unconfirmed, IRGC Commander-in-Chief Hossein Salami appears on state television as acting head of state, vowing that Iran will continue to strike American and Israeli targets until the aggression ceases. He calls on all regional allies to join the fight.
factCheckNote: Broadcast on IRIB state television; Salami's assumption of acting authority noted by multiple diplomatic sources

title: Second Wave of US Strikes Hits IRGC Command Centers
description: The US launches a second major wave of strikes targeting IRGC command-and-control facilities, communications nodes, and missile production sites. B-1B Lancers and carrier-based F/A-18 Super Hornets strike over 60 targets across western and central Iran. The Pentagon reports that Iran's ability to coordinate further missile attacks is significantly degraded.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM releases operational summary with target list; satellite imagery confirms strikes on known IRGC facilities

title: China and Russia Call for Restraint, Block UN Resolution
description: China and Russia issue a joint statement condemning the US military action as 'a reckless escalation' and veto a proposed UN Security Council resolution that would have authorized international intervention. Beijing offers to mediate but Washington dismisses the proposal.
factCheckNote: UN Security Council voting record is public; joint statement released through official Chinese and Russian diplomatic channels

title: US Carrier Strike Group Repositions in Arabian Sea
description: The USS Dwight D. Eisenhower carrier strike group repositions from the Gulf of Oman to the northern Arabian Sea to provide additional air support and missile defense coverage. A second carrier, USS Harry S. Truman, is ordered to accelerate its transit from the Mediterranean to reinforce operations.
factCheckNote: US Navy confirms repositioning; commercial ship tracking data and satellite imagery corroborate carrier movements

title: Hezbollah Launches 150+ Rockets from Southern Lebanon into Israel
description: Hezbollah opens a second front by launching over 150 rockets and guided missiles from southern Lebanon into northern Israel, striking Haifa, Tiberias, and military installations in the Galilee. Israel's Iron Dome and David's Sling systems are overwhelmed in some sectors, with several rockets striking urban areas.
factCheckNote: IDF confirms incoming rocket count; UNIFIL forces in southern Lebanon report launch activity; damage in Israeli cities documented by journalists

narratives.us: Hezbollah's attack proves it is an Iranian proxy doing Tehran's bidding, opening a terrorist front against Israeli civilians
narratives.iran: The Lebanese resistance is standing in solidarity with Iran against the US-Israeli axis of aggression, as is their right

title: Israel Retaliates with Airstrikes on Beirut Suburbs
description: The Israeli Air Force launches heavy airstrikes on Hezbollah command centers and weapons depots in the Dahieh suburb of Beirut. Lebanese authorities report significant destruction in densely populated areas. At least 28 Lebanese civilians are reported killed, drawing international condemnation.
factCheckNote: IDF confirms strikes and provides target justification; Lebanese Red Cross confirms civilian casualties; destruction documented by local and international media

title: Iran Begins Mining Strait of Hormuz with Naval Mines
description: Iranian naval forces and IRGC Navy fast boats are observed deploying naval mines in shipping lanes near the Strait of Hormuz. The US Navy's 5th Fleet issues a navigational warning to all commercial vessels. The move threatens to choke off approximately 21 million barrels of oil per day that transit the narrow waterway.
factCheckNote: US Navy surveillance confirms mine-laying activity; however, full extent and type of mines deployed has not been independently verified

narratives.us: Mining international shipping lanes is an act of economic terrorism that threatens the global economy and the energy security of every nation
narratives.iran: Iran has the sovereign right to defend its territorial waters and deny the enemy use of strategic waterways during wartime

title: IRINS Dena Frigate Sunk by US Navy in Gulf Engagement
description: The Iranian frigate IRINS Dena is sunk in a naval engagement with the US destroyer USS Laboon in the Persian Gulf after the Iranian vessel allegedly targeted a US minesweeper with anti-ship missiles. The engagement lasts approximately 20 minutes, with the Dena taking multiple Harpoon missile hits before sinking. Iran reports 43 sailors missing.
factCheckNote: US Navy provides detailed engagement timeline; satellite imagery confirms wreckage location; Iranian naval sources acknowledge loss of vessel

narratives.us: The USS Laboon acted in self-defense after the Iranian frigate fired on coalition minesweeping operations in international waters
narratives.iran: The Americans destroyed a naval vessel conducting legitimate defense operations in Iranian territorial waters

title: Oil Tanker MV Pacific Dawn Struck by Mine Near Hormuz
description: The Liberian-flagged oil tanker MV Pacific Dawn strikes a naval mine approximately 15 nautical miles from the Strait of Hormuz, causing a massive hull breach and an oil spill in the Gulf of Oman. The crew of 24 is evacuated by nearby vessels. The incident causes Brent crude to spike to $128 per barrel.
factCheckNote: Distress signal logged by maritime authorities; crew rescued and interviewed; ship tracking data confirms location; oil slick visible on satellite imagery

title: EU Announces Emergency Energy Summit
description: European Commission President calls an emergency EU energy summit as European gas and oil prices surge to record levels. EU members begin discussing activation of strategic petroleum reserves and accelerated negotiations with alternative suppliers in Africa and the Americas.
factCheckNote: EU press office confirms summit scheduling; official statements from multiple EU heads of state

title: Humanitarian Corridors Proposed for Civilian Evacuation
description: The International Committee of the Red Cross proposes establishing humanitarian corridors to evacuate civilians from conflict zones in Iran, Lebanon, and Israel. The ICRC calls on all parties to respect international humanitarian law and allow safe passage for civilians and humanitarian workers.
factCheckNote: Official ICRC press release and statements from Geneva headquarters; proposal acknowledged by all parties

title: Strait of Hormuz Declared Unsafe for Navigation
description: The Combined Maritime Forces and the International Maritime Organization jointly declare the Strait of Hormuz unsafe for commercial navigation due to confirmed mine fields and ongoing military operations. Major shipping companies including Maersk, MSC, and CMA CGM suspend all transits through the strait indefinitely.
factCheckNote: Official IMO navigation warning issued; major shipping companies confirm suspension of transits through public statements

title: Qatar Halts LNG Exports Through Hormuz
description: Qatar, the world's largest LNG exporter, announces a temporary halt to all liquefied natural gas shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. The decision sends European natural gas futures soaring by 32% as the continent faces the prospect of severe energy shortages heading into late winter.
factCheckNote: QatarEnergy issues official statement; European gas futures data confirmed by ICE exchange; vessel tracking shows LNG carriers diverting or anchoring

title: Global Stock Markets Crash — Dow Drops 1,200 Points at Open
description: Global financial markets plunge as the economic consequences of the Iran conflict intensify. The Dow Jones Industrial Average drops 1,200 points in the first hour of trading, the S&P 500 falls 5.3%, and the NASDAQ drops 6.1%. Circuit breakers are triggered on multiple European exchanges.
factCheckNote: Real-time market data from NYSE, NASDAQ, and European exchanges; trading volumes and price movements independently verifiable

title: Iraq-Based Militias Fire Rockets at US Forces at Al Asad Base
description: Iran-backed Iraqi militias, including Kata'ib Hezbollah and Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq, launch a sustained rocket and drone barrage against Al Asad Air Base in western Iraq. The base sustains moderate damage and two US soldiers are wounded. Iraqi government condemns the attack but is unable to prevent it.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM confirms attack and casualties; Iraqi security forces corroborate; militia groups claim responsibility on Telegram channels

narratives.us: Iran's proxy network is attacking American forces across the region, confirming the need to dismantle the IRGC's entire terror infrastructure
narratives.iran: The Iraqi resistance movements are acting independently in solidarity with the people of Iran against American occupation forces

title: Trump Sanctions All Nations Trading with Iran
description: President Trump signs an executive order imposing secondary sanctions on any nation or entity conducting trade with Iran, including oil purchases, financial transactions, and arms transfers. The move is aimed primarily at China and India, which are major Iranian oil importers. Beijing calls the sanctions 'economic bullying.'
factCheckNote: Executive order published in Federal Register; Treasury Department issues detailed guidance; affected nations acknowledge new sanctions

title: Reports of Iranian Civilian Casualties Mount — Hospitals Overwhelmed
description: Iranian medical officials report that hospitals in Tehran, Isfahan, and Shiraz are overwhelmed with civilian casualties from the ongoing US and Israeli airstrikes. The Iranian Red Crescent reports over 400 civilian deaths and thousands wounded. Aid organizations struggle to reach affected areas due to destroyed infrastructure.
factCheckNote: Iranian Red Crescent figures cited but cannot be independently verified due to restricted media access; some hospital reports corroborated by WHO staff in Iran

narratives.us: The US military takes extraordinary measures to minimize civilian casualties; Iran bears responsibility for placing military assets in civilian areas
narratives.iran: America's so-called 'precision strikes' have killed hundreds of innocent Iranian civilians, constituting war crimes that the world must condemn

title: UK Royal Navy Deploys Additional Minesweepers to Gulf
description: The UK Ministry of Defence announces the deployment of two additional Hunt-class minesweepers and a support vessel to the Persian Gulf to assist with mine clearance operations near the Strait of Hormuz. France and Australia pledge naval assets to the multinational minesweeping effort.
factCheckNote: UK MoD official press release; vessel movements confirmed by ship tracking data and allied navy communications

title: Qatari F-15 Mistakenly Enters Combat Zone, Shot Down
description: A Qatari Air Force F-15QA Eagle reportedly strays into an active combat zone over the Persian Gulf and is shot down under unclear circumstances. Qatar claims the aircraft was on a routine patrol and demands an investigation. The pilot ejects safely and is rescued by Qatari naval forces.
factCheckNote: Qatar confirms loss of aircraft but provides no details on circumstances; both US and Iran deny responsibility; investigation ongoing

narratives.us: The incident is under investigation; the fog of war creates tragic situations and we are working with Qatari allies to determine what happened
narratives.iran: This incident proves that American recklessness is endangering even their own Gulf allies and destabilizing the entire region

title: Azerbaijan's Oil Infrastructure Struck by Iranian Missiles
description: Iranian missiles strike oil infrastructure in southern Azerbaijan, hitting a pipeline terminal near Baku. Iran accuses Azerbaijan of allowing Israeli intelligence operations on its territory and warns of further strikes if Azerbaijan continues to cooperate with Israel. Azerbaijan recalls its ambassador from Tehran.
factCheckNote: Azerbaijani government confirms damage to infrastructure; Iran has not claimed responsibility but launched threatening statements; missile trajectory analysis ongoing

title: Oil Markets Crash — Brent Crude Surges Past $85/Barrel, Gas Prices Beginning to Rise
description: Crude oil prices surge past $85/barrel as the Hormuz blockade takes hold. US national average gas prices remain near $3.12/gallon (AAA) as pump prices lag crude by 1-2 weeks, but analysts project sharp increases ahead. Wholesale gasoline futures spike, signaling imminent price increases. States prepare emergency price gouging protections.
factCheckNote: AAA and GasBuddy data confirm national average; state-level data corroborated by EIA weekly petroleum status report

title: Iran Claims to Have Shot Down US MQ-9 Reaper Drone
description: Iranian state media broadcasts footage purporting to show the wreckage of a US MQ-9 Reaper drone shot down over Khuzestan province by Iranian air defenses. The Pentagon states it is 'assessing reports' but does not confirm the loss. Independent analysts note the footage is inconclusive.
factCheckNote: Only source is Iranian state media (IRIB/Press TV); wreckage footage is low quality and cannot be conclusively identified as MQ-9; Pentagon has not confirmed

title: Pope Francis Makes Urgent Peace Appeal
description: Pope Francis delivers an impassioned address from the Vatican calling on all parties to cease hostilities immediately and pursue peace through dialogue. He offers the Vatican as a neutral venue for peace negotiations and warns that the conflict threatens to plunge the world into a catastrophe of unprecedented scale.
factCheckNote: Vatican press office releases full transcript; address broadcast live by Vatican Media and covered by all major international outlets

title: Israel Launches New Wave of Strikes on Iranian Missile Sites
description: The Israeli Air Force conducts a major overnight strike package targeting Iranian ballistic missile production facilities and mobile launcher staging areas in western Iran. Israel reports destroying at least 12 Shahab-3 transporter-erector-launchers and a major missile assembly plant near Kermanshah.
factCheckNote: IDF releases satellite imagery of struck targets; independent OSINT analysts confirm damage at identified locations through commercial satellite comparison

title: Iran Declares 'Long War' Strategy, Mobilizes Reserves
description: Iran's acting Supreme National Security Council announces a shift to a 'long war' strategy, calling up military reserves and ordering the mobilization of Basij paramilitary forces numbering in the hundreds of thousands. Iranian officials declare that the nation will fight an asymmetric war of attrition designed to exhaust the American-Israeli alliance.
factCheckNote: Official statement carried on IRIB state television and confirmed by IRGC-affiliated Tasnim News Agency; mobilization orders corroborated by social media posts from reservists

narratives.us: Iran's desperation is showing — shifting to a 'long war' strategy is an admission that their conventional military capability has been severely degraded
narratives.iran: Iran is a nation of 88 million people with a 5,000-year history of resisting invaders — the Americans will learn that occupying Iran is impossible

title: Houthi Rebels Attack US Navy Destroyer in Red Sea
description: Yemen's Houthi rebels launch a coordinated attack on the USS Gravely, an Arleigh Burke-class destroyer operating in the southern Red Sea, using a combination of anti-ship ballistic missiles and explosive-laden drones. The Gravely intercepts all incoming threats using its Aegis combat system but expends a significant portion of its missile inventory.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM confirms the engagement; Houthi military spokesperson claims responsibility; commercial ships in the area report witnessing interceptions

title: DOE Releases 30M Barrels from Strategic Petroleum Reserve as Crude Hits $95
description: With Brent crude reaching $95/barrel, the Department of Energy announces the release of 30 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. US national average gas at $3.22/gallon (AAA) — pump prices lag crude by 1-2 weeks but projected to reach $4+/gal. Analysts warn SPR release provides only temporary relief without Hormuz reopening.
factCheckNote: AAA and EIA data confirm price; DOE press release confirms SPR release authorization

title: Unconfirmed Reports of Iranian Special Forces in Iraq
description: Multiple unverified reports emerge from Iraqi Kurdish sources claiming that Iranian Quds Force operatives have crossed into Iraq's Kurdistan region, potentially to establish forward staging areas for asymmetric operations against US forces. The Iraqi government denies any unauthorized Iranian military presence on its soil.
factCheckNote: Reports originate from Kurdish Peshmerga intelligence sources; unable to independently verify due to remote terrain and lack of journalist access to border areas

title: Overnight US Strikes Target Iranian Naval Bases at Bandar Abbas
description: US forces conduct a major overnight strike on Iran's primary naval base at Bandar Abbas, targeting IRGC Navy fast attack craft, midget submarines, and coastal missile batteries. The strikes aim to neutralize Iran's ability to continue mining operations and threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM releases post-strike assessment with satellite imagery; multiple defense correspondents briefed by Pentagon officials confirm operational details

title: Iran Fires Ballistic Missiles at US Base in Bahrain
description: Iran launches a volley of medium-range ballistic missiles at Naval Support Activity Bahrain, the headquarters of the US 5th Fleet. Bahrain's Patriot batteries and US naval Aegis systems engage the incoming missiles. Early reports suggest most were intercepted, but at least one missile impacts near the base perimeter.
factCheckNote: Bahraini civil defense confirms missile alerts and interceptions; US 5th Fleet acknowledges 'inbound threat engagement' but has not released detailed damage assessment

narratives.us: Iran is spreading the war to Gulf states that have nothing to do with the conflict, endangering civilians throughout the region
narratives.iran: Any nation that hosts American military forces used to attack Iran is a legitimate target and complicit in the aggression

title: Brent Crude Tops $100/Barrel — Pump Prices Climbing, Analysts Warn of $4+/Gallon
description: Brent crude breaks through $100/barrel for the first time since the Hormuz blockade began. US national average gas at $3.28/gallon (AAA) and rising. Pump prices lag crude oil by 1-2 weeks. GasBuddy's Patrick De Haan projects $3.50-3.65 before stabilizing; Eurasia Group's Ian Bremmer warns of $4/gal next week. Long lines at gas stations in major cities as consumers engage in panic buying.
factCheckNote: AAA, GasBuddy, and EIA data all confirm national average; state-level price gouging orders are public record

title: CENTCOM: 'Operations Proceeding Ahead of Schedule'
description: CENTCOM Commander General delivers a press briefing stating that Operation Epic Fury is 'proceeding ahead of schedule' with over 80% of identified nuclear targets destroyed. He reports that Iran's ballistic missile capability has been reduced by approximately 60% and its air defense network is effectively neutralized.
factCheckNote: Official CENTCOM press briefing attended by credentialed journalists; operational claims consistent with available satellite imagery and independent damage assessments

title: Reports of Major Cyberattack on Iranian Power Grid
description: Multiple regions across Iran experience widespread power outages as reports emerge of a sophisticated cyberattack targeting the country's electrical grid control systems. Iranian officials blame the US and Israel for the attack while attempting to restore power to major cities including Tehran, Mashhad, and Tabriz.
factCheckNote: Power outages confirmed by Iranian citizens on social media and satellite nightlight imagery; attribution to cyberattack is claimed by Iran but not independently confirmed — could also be result of kinetic strikes on infrastructure

title: B-2 Stealth Bombers Strike Iranian Underground Nuclear Facilities
description: US B-2 Spirit stealth bombers deployed GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators against deeply buried nuclear enrichment facilities at Isfahan and Natanz. The strikes targeted reinforced underground chambers believed to house centrifuge cascades and enriched uranium stockpiles. CENTCOM reported secondary explosions at both sites, suggesting stored nuclear materials or equipment were hit.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM releases post-strike satellite imagery showing surface cratering consistent with deep-penetration munitions; seismic data from regional monitoring stations corroborates underground detonations

narratives.us: These strikes are specifically designed to eliminate Iran's ability to develop nuclear weapons — the primary objective of Operation Epic Fury
narratives.iran: America has attacked civilian nuclear energy infrastructure in violation of international law, risking radiological contamination of populated areas

title: Israel Strikes Hezbollah Command Center in Beirut Suburb
description: Israeli Air Force conducted a precision strike on a Hezbollah underground command-and-control center in the Dahieh suburb of Beirut. Lebanese health ministry reported 23 killed including civilians in surrounding buildings. Israel claimed multiple senior Hezbollah military planners were eliminated in the strike, which used penetration munitions to reach the hardened bunker beneath residential buildings.
factCheckNote: IDF confirms the strike and releases aerial footage; Lebanese health ministry casualty figures corroborated by hospital records; identity of killed Hezbollah commanders not independently verified

narratives.us: Israel is conducting legitimate self-defense operations against a designated terrorist organization that has been attacking Israeli territory
narratives.iran: The Zionist regime is committing war crimes by bombing civilian neighborhoods in Lebanon, killing innocent women and children

title: Iran Launches Retaliatory Ballistic Missiles at US Base in Bahrain
description: In retaliation for the B-2 strikes, Iran's IRGC fired a salvo of Fateh-313 and Dezful medium-range ballistic missiles at Naval Support Activity Bahrain, headquarters of the US 5th Fleet. Patriot and THAAD systems intercepted most incoming projectiles, but at least two missiles impacted within the base perimeter. Early reports indicated US casualties.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM confirms inbound missile engagement and casualties; Bahraini civil defense corroborates with siren activation and shelter-in-place orders; missile debris recovered and identified

narratives.us: This unprovoked attack on a US military installation in a sovereign Gulf state is a dangerous escalation that endangers the entire region
narratives.iran: Any base used by American forces to launch attacks against the Islamic Republic is a legitimate military target under international law

title: Massive Cyber Attack Cripples Iranian Banking System
description: A sophisticated cyber operation disabled Iran's SHETAB interbank payment network, taking ATMs offline nationwide and freezing electronic financial transactions. Millions of Iranian citizens were left unable to access funds or purchase basic necessities. The attack was widely attributed to Israel's Unit 8200 cyber warfare division, though no official claim of responsibility was made.
factCheckNote: Banking system outage confirmed by Iranian citizens on social media and Iranian banking association statement; attribution to Israel based on technical analysis by cybersecurity firms but not officially confirmed

title: Red Cross Requests Humanitarian Corridor for Tehran Civilians
description: The International Committee of the Red Cross formally requested all belligerents establish a humanitarian corridor to allow civilian evacuation from Tehran's heavily bombarded southern districts. ICRC reported hospitals overwhelmed with casualties, critical shortages of medical supplies and blood, and an estimated 500,000 internally displaced persons within the capital alone.
factCheckNote: Official ICRC press release issued from Geneva; WHO staff in Iran corroborate hospital conditions; displacement estimates consistent with satellite imagery of population movement

title: Oil Prices Surge Past $90/Barrel as Escalation Deepens
description: Brent crude surged past $90 per barrel for the first time since the conflict began, hitting $91.40 intraday as the B-2 bunker-buster campaign and Iranian retaliation against Bahrain signaled a deepening spiral of escalation. WTI crude reached $88.75. Goldman Sachs issued a warning that oil could reach $120/barrel if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed through April.
factCheckNote: ICE and NYMEX exchange data confirm price movements; Goldman Sachs research note publicly available; US gas prices confirmed by AAA

title: UN Security Council Emergency Session — US and UK Veto Ceasefire Resolution
description: The UN Security Council convened an emergency session following the Bahrain strike and B-2 bombing campaign. Russia and China introduced a joint resolution demanding an immediate ceasefire from all parties. The United States and United Kingdom vetoed the resolution, arguing it failed to address Iran's nuclear weapons program. France abstained. The Secretary-General made a direct appeal for an immediate cessation of hostilities.
factCheckNote: UN proceedings are publicly documented; voting records published by the Security Council; transcripts of statements available from UN press office

narratives.us: A ceasefire that leaves Iran's nuclear weapons infrastructure intact is unacceptable — the resolution was fundamentally flawed and one-sided
narratives.iran: America and Britain have once again shown their contempt for international law by blocking a ceasefire while they bomb our nation

title: Anti-War Protests Erupt Across Major US Cities
description: Large-scale anti-war protests erupted in Washington D.C., New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, and San Francisco, with tens of thousands marching to demand an end to military operations against Iran. In Washington, demonstrators attempted to reach the White House gates before being turned back by riot police. Several members of Congress joined the protests. Smaller solidarity demonstrations were held in London, Berlin, and Paris.
factCheckNote: Multiple news organizations confirm protests with live coverage; police departments in respective cities confirm crowd estimates; arrest records publicly available

title: Hezbollah Drone Strikes British RAF Base at Akrotiri, Cyprus
description: A Hezbollah-launched drone struck the British Royal Air Force base at Akrotiri in Cyprus, damaging the runway. The attack represented a significant escalation, marking the first strike on European sovereign territory in the conflict. French President Macron declared that an 'attack on Cyprus is an attack on all Europe,' raising the specter of broader European involvement in the conflict.
factCheckNote: UK Ministry of Defence confirms damage to RAF Akrotiri; Macron statement issued by Elysee Palace and covered by all major European news outlets

narratives.us: Iran's proxies are now attacking NATO territory, demonstrating the global threat posed by the Iranian regime and its allies
narratives.iran: Any military installation used to support aggression against the resistance axis is a legitimate target regardless of location

title: Israel Launches 'Wide-Scale' Strikes on Tehran Energy Infrastructure
description: The IDF announced a new phase of operations targeting Iranian regime energy resources, launching wide-scale strikes on Tehran's energy infrastructure including refineries, fuel depots, and power generation facilities. The strikes aimed to cripple Iran's ability to sustain its war effort by degrading its energy supply chain.
factCheckNote: IDF official statement confirmed strikes; satellite imagery from commercial providers shows fires at multiple energy facilities in Tehran; PBS and international media independently confirmed

narratives.us: Targeting energy infrastructure is a legitimate military objective to degrade Iran's ability to wage war and fund its proxy networks
narratives.iran: The deliberate destruction of civilian energy infrastructure is a war crime that will plunge millions of Iranians into darkness and cold

title: Iranian Drone Damages Bahrain Desalination Plant — 1 Dead
description: A hostile drone struck the Salman Industrial City in Bahrain, damaging a desalination plant and killing one person. This marked the first infrastructure attack on Bahrain in the conflict, escalating concerns about the vulnerability of Gulf state civilian infrastructure to Iranian drone warfare.
factCheckNote: Bahrain Ministry of Interior confirmed the strike; damage to desalination facility corroborated by satellite imagery and local media

title: Saudi Arabia Reports First Civilian Deaths from Iranian Strikes
description: A projectile struck a residential area in Al-Kharj, near Riyadh, killing 2 civilians and wounding 12 in what the Saudi Ministry of Defense described as the first civilian deaths on Saudi soil from Iranian strikes. The attack marked a dangerous new dimension in the conflict, directly threatening Gulf state populations.
factCheckNote: Saudi Ministry of Defense statement confirmed casualties; hospital records corroborate wounded figures; debris analysis underway

narratives.us: Iran's reckless attacks are now killing civilians in allied Gulf nations, proving the regime's complete disregard for innocent life
narratives.iran: We have apologized to Gulf nations and indicated these attacks will end; our conflict is with the American and Zionist aggressors

title: 8th US Service Member Killed in Iran War
description: A US soldier died from injuries sustained during Iranian strikes on a base in Saudi Arabia, bringing the total US military deaths in the conflict to eight. President Trump attended the dignified transfer ceremony at Dover Air Force Base, the first time he has personally received remains since the war began.
factCheckNote: Department of Defense confirmed the casualty; White House pool reporters documented Trump's presence at Dover AFB dignified transfer

title: Netanyahu Announces Wide-Scale Strikes on Iranian Regime Targets
description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israel would continue wide-scale strikes on Iranian regime targets until the threat is eliminated. He stated that the IDF is operating in Tehran and additional areas, signaling an expansion of Israel's target list beyond nuclear and military infrastructure to include government and energy assets.
factCheckNote: Official statement from Israeli PM's office; broadcast live on Israeli television and covered by all major international outlets

title: Mojtaba Khamenei Named Iran's New Supreme Leader
description: Iran's Assembly of Experts elected Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as the new Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic. The IRGC immediately pledged allegiance to the new leader, who vowed that Iran would not rest until every aggressor has been punished. The appointment signaled continuity in Iran's hardline stance and rejection of any negotiated settlement.
factCheckNote: Assembly of Experts proceedings confirmed by Iranian state media; IRGC allegiance statement broadcast on IRIB; covered by CNN, Al Jazeera, and all major outlets

narratives.us: The appointment of Khamenei's son shows the regime is a hereditary dictatorship, not a republic, and will continue its dangerous nuclear ambitions
narratives.iran: The selection of Mojtaba Khamenei ensures the continuation of the Supreme Leader's sacred mission to defend Islam and resist Western imperialism

title: Swiss Government Declares US-Israel Attacks Violate International Law
description: Swiss Defense Minister Martin Pfister issued a formal statement declaring that Switzerland believes the attacks on Iran constitute a violation of international law. The statement asserted that all parties — the United States, Israel, and Iran — have broken international law's prohibition on the use of force. The declaration was notable as Switzerland is a traditionally neutral country and hosts the Geneva Conventions.
factCheckNote: Official statement issued by Swiss Federal Council; published on Swiss government website and covered by European media outlets

title: Trump Demands Iran's 'Unconditional Surrender' — Iran Rejects Ceasefire
description: President Trump demanded Iran's unconditional surrender, stating that short-term oil price increases are a 'very small price to pay' for destroying Iran's nuclear threat. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi rejected any ceasefire overtures, denying the existence of behind-the-scenes contacts with the United States and vowing that Iran would respond proportionally to every aggression.
factCheckNote: Trump statement confirmed by White House press pool; Araghchi statement broadcast on Iranian state television and reported by NPR, Al Jazeera

narratives.us: Only unconditional surrender will guarantee that Iran never develops nuclear weapons — half-measures and diplomatic agreements have failed for decades
narratives.iran: Iran will never surrender to American imperialism. We are a proud nation with 7,000 years of civilization that will not bow to threats

title: Oil Smashes Through $100/Barrel — Strait of Hormuz Effectively Closed
description: Brent crude shattered the $100/barrel barrier, hitting $119.50 intraday in the biggest weekly surge since 1983. The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatened approximately 20% of the world's oil supply. Energy analysts warned of sustained triple-digit oil prices if the conflict continues, with cascading effects on global inflation, food prices, and economic growth.
factCheckNote: ICE exchange data confirms Brent crude price movements; shipping industry tracking data confirms effective Hormuz closure; Bloomberg and CNBC independently reported

title: Iran's Death Toll Surpasses 1,332 — Over 6,000 Wounded
description: Iran's death toll from the US-Israeli military campaign surpassed 1,332 with over 6,000 wounded, according to figures from the Iranian Red Crescent and international humanitarian organizations. At least 300 children were reported hospitalized. The Red Crescent reported an escalating humanitarian crisis with overwhelmed hospitals, critical shortages of medical supplies, and millions of internally displaced persons.
factCheckNote: Casualty figures reported by Iranian Red Crescent and cited by Al Jazeera; exact numbers difficult to independently verify due to limited journalist access; WHO corroborates hospital overload reports

title: Senate War Powers Resolution Fails — Trump Authorized to Continue Strikes
description: The US Senate voted down a war powers resolution that would have forced President Trump to withdraw from military operations against Iran. The resolution's failure gives the administration indefinite authorization to continue the campaign. Several Republican senators who had initially expressed reservations ultimately voted against the measure.
factCheckNote: Senate vote recorded and publicly available; covered by CNBC, AP, and all major outlets

title: IDF Chief Warns 'No Safe Place' for Iranian Regime — Lebanon Attacks Exceed Iran's
description: The IDF Chief of Staff warned that there is 'no safe place' for the Iranian regime as the war entered its second week. Israeli military officials also revealed that Lebanon (Hezbollah) has conducted more attacks against Israel than Iran itself over the past week, highlighting how the conflict has expanded into a multi-front war.
factCheckNote: IDF official briefing confirmed by Israeli media; attack comparison statistics from IDF operational data

title: Fake AI Satellite Imagery Fuels War Disinformation — Millions of Views
description: AFP confirmed that an Iranian news outlet posted AI-generated fake satellite imagery purporting to show a devastated US base in Qatar. The fabricated image contained gibberish coordinates and was identified via Google's SynthID invisible watermark. The image garnered millions of views across Instagram, Threads, and X in multiple languages, illustrating the growing threat of AI-powered wartime disinformation.
factCheckNote: AFP detected SynthID watermark; analysis confirmed fabrication; covered by Arab News, Manila Times, and multiple fact-checking organizations

title: Trump: 'We've Already Won in Many Ways' — Calls New Supreme Leader a 'Lightweight'
description: President Trump told The Times of Israel that the US has 'already won in many ways, but we haven't won enough.' He called the new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei a 'lightweight' who 'would not last long without his approval,' and said he must have a role in choosing Iran's next leader. Trump characterized the war as a 'short-term excursion' and said ending it would be a 'mutual' decision with Netanyahu.
factCheckNote: Direct quotes from Trump's phone interview with Times of Israel; corroborated by CBS News, Washington Post

narratives.us: Victory is within reach — the Iranian regime's nuclear threat has been effectively neutralized and a new democratic future for Iran is possible
narratives.iran: Trump's arrogant statements about choosing Iran's leader prove this is a colonial war of regime change, not self-defense

title: Iran Claims Capture of US RQ-4 Global Hawk Drone Over Persian Gulf
description: Iran's IRGC Navy announced it had intercepted and captured a US RQ-4 Global Hawk surveillance drone operating over the northern Persian Gulf. CENTCOM has not confirmed the claim. If verified, this would be the second time Iran has downed a US Global Hawk, after the June 2019 incident that nearly triggered US strikes.
factCheckNote: IRGC released footage of drone wreckage; CENTCOM neither confirmed nor denied the loss

title: IRGC Drone Strikes Hit Oil Tankers Prima and Louise P in Persian Gulf
description: IRGC naval forces claimed strikes on the oil tanker MV Prima in the central Persian Gulf and the US-flagged oil tanker Louise P near the Strait of Hormuz using armed drones. Both vessels reported damage but remained afloat. The attacks brought the total of commercial vessels damaged in the Hormuz blockade to 8.
factCheckNote: Maritime tracking data confirmed both vessels took evasive action; satellite imagery showed damage consistent with drone strikes

title: Hegseth: Iran Has Fired 500+ Ballistic Missiles and 2,000 Drones — 'Iranian Navy Is Largely No More'
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated at a Pentagon briefing that Iran has fired over 500 ballistic missiles and 2,000 drones since the conflict began on February 28. He declared that the Iranian Navy is 'largely no more' and signaled a 4-8 week campaign timeline. The statements underscored the scale of both the Iranian retaliation and US-Israeli military destruction.
factCheckNote: Hegseth statements confirmed by Pentagon transcript; missile/drone counts broadly consistent with CENTCOM intercept data though exact figures not independently verifiable; Iranian Navy losses confirmed by satellite imagery

title: Araghchi Rejects Ceasefire on NBC — 'We Need to Continue Fighting'
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi appeared on NBC News and rejected any ceasefire, stating 'We need to continue fighting for the sake of our people.' He said Iran will not negotiate under fire and denied any behind-the-scenes contacts with the United States. The statement indicated Iran's intent to sustain military operations despite significant losses.
factCheckNote: Araghchi interview broadcast on NBC and clipped by multiple outlets; NPR and Al Jazeera independently reported the statements

title: Bahrain Hit by Iranian Missiles — 32 Wounded, 1 Killed
description: Iranian missiles struck Bahrain, wounding 32 people including children in Sitra and killing a woman in a separate strike on a residential area in Manama. The attack represented a significant escalation of Iranian strikes on Gulf state civilian areas, coming days after the earlier desalination plant strike and Saudi civilian deaths.
factCheckNote: Bahrain Ministry of Interior confirmed casualties; hospital records corroborate; covered by Al Jazeera and PBS

narratives.us: Iran's continued strikes on Bahraini civilians demonstrate the regime's indiscriminate targeting of Gulf populations
narratives.iran: Bahrain hosts the US 5th Fleet and is a combatant in this war — civilian areas near military targets are an unfortunate consequence

title: Turkey: NATO Air Defenses Shoot Down Iranian Ballistic Missile
description: NATO air defense systems in Turkey intercepted and destroyed an Iranian ballistic missile that entered Turkish airspace. This was the second such interception over Turkey in the past week, significantly raising the stakes for a potential NATO Article 5 invocation. Turkey also deployed 6 F-16s and additional air defense assets to Northern Cyprus in response.
factCheckNote: NATO confirmed the interception; Turkish military deployment reported by Anadolu Agency and Reuters; Article 5 discussions acknowledged by NATO spokesperson

narratives.us: Iranian missiles violating NATO airspace prove the global threat this regime poses and why military action was necessary
narratives.iran: Turkey and NATO are complicit in the aggression against Iran — their airspace was used for attack operations

title: Lebanon Proposes Direct Peace Talks With Israel — Both US and Israel Reject
description: Lebanon proposed direct peace negotiations with Israel in an effort to de-escalate the multi-front conflict. Both the United States and Israel rejected the offer, citing the ongoing Iranian threat and Hezbollah's continued attacks from Lebanese territory as preconditions that must be addressed first.
factCheckNote: Lebanese government statement confirmed by Reuters; US and Israeli rejections covered by Al Jazeera and PBS

title: US Gas Hits $3.54/Gallon — Diesel Surges to $4.66/Gallon
description: US gasoline prices continued their rapid ascent, with the national average hitting $3.54 per gallon and diesel surging to $4.66 per gallon — up 89 cents in a single week, the biggest weekly fuel price increase on record. The surge was driven by the Strait of Hormuz closure and Brent crude prices above $100/barrel.
factCheckNote: AAA fuel price data confirms national averages; GasBuddy corroborates weekly increase figures

title: House Votes Against War Powers Resolution 212-219
description: The US House of Representatives voted 212-219 against a war powers resolution that would have compelled President Trump to withdraw from military operations against Iran. Combined with the Senate's 47-53 defeat, Congress has no check on the ongoing military campaign. The administration now has indefinite authorization to continue operations.
factCheckNote: House vote publicly recorded; CNBC and AP News confirmed the final tally

title: 40 Killed in Airstrike on Residential Buildings in Eastern Tehran
description: A US-Israeli airstrike on residential buildings in eastern Tehran killed at least 40 people. Earlier strikes on oil facilities blanketed the capital in toxic smoke. Iran's deputy health minister confirmed over 1,255 killed and 12,000 wounded across 11 days of war. The strike was one of the deadliest single attacks on civilian areas since the conflict began.
factCheckNote: Iranian state media and Al Jazeera confirmed the death toll from the residential buildings attack; hospital records corroborate mass casualties

title: Hegseth Warns Tomorrow Will Be 'Most Intense Day of Strikes' Yet
description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warned that Tuesday (Day 12) will be the most intense day of strikes against Iran since the war began, signaling a major escalation of the aerial campaign. The statement came as CENTCOM confirmed over 3,000 targets struck in Iran since February 28.
factCheckNote: Hegseth statement from Pentagon briefing; PBS and Al Jazeera independently confirmed

title: Iran Vows 'Eye for an Eye' — Threatens Energy Infrastructure Retaliation
description: Iran vowed an 'eye for an eye' response to continued US-Israeli strikes on Iranian oil and energy infrastructure. A senior Iranian official told CNN that Israel's attacks on oil and fuel depots have pushed the war into a 'new phase' and threatened retaliatory strikes on energy infrastructure in Israel and Gulf states.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera live blog confirmed the statement; CNN separately reported the senior Iranian official's comments

narratives.us: Iran's threats against energy infrastructure prove why their military capabilities must be completely neutralized
narratives.iran: If the enemy strikes our oil, we will strike theirs — this is self-defense under international law

title: China, Russia, France Contact Iran Regarding Ceasefire
description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi confirmed that China, Russia, and France have contacted Iran regarding a possible ceasefire. However, Iran's Foreign Ministry reiterated that Tehran has no interest in negotiations while under attack. The diplomatic outreach signals growing international concern about the conflict's trajectory and economic fallout.
factCheckNote: Gharibabadi's statement reported by Al Jazeera and Reuters; individual country contacts corroborated by European diplomatic sources

title: Isfahan UNESCO Heritage Sites Damaged — Shah Mosque, Naqsh-e Jahan Hit
description: Strikes on Isfahan damaged multiple UNESCO World Heritage sites including Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Chehel Sotoun palace, Ali Qapu, the Shah Mosque, and Teymouri Hall. The destruction of irreplaceable cultural heritage drew condemnation from UNESCO and cultural organizations worldwide.
factCheckNote: Damage confirmed by Iranian state media and OSINT satellite imagery; Al Jazeera and Britannica independently reported

title: CENTCOM: 5,000+ Targets Struck — 90% Reduction in Iranian Missile Launches
description: Joint Chiefs Chairman Dan Caine confirmed the US military has struck over 5,000 targets since February 28, achieving a 90% reduction in Iran's missile launches and 83% decrease in drone attacks. Defense Secretary Hegseth announced March 10 would be the 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran' yet. 16 Iranian naval minelayers destroyed near the Strait of Hormuz and a second IRGC warship destroyed at Bandar Abbas.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM and Pentagon press briefing figures confirmed by CBS News and NPR

title: Iran Uses Cluster Munitions Against Israeli Civilians — 300 Missiles Fired
description: IDF confirmed Iran has fired approximately 300 missiles at Israel since the war began, with about half carrying cluster bomb warheads banned under the 2008 Convention on Cluster Munitions. A cluster munition strike on central Israel killed 2 people and seriously wounded 1, hitting 6 sites. 186 total attack waves have been launched against Israel.
factCheckNote: IDF assessment confirmed by Times of Israel; cluster munition impacts verified by OSINT imagery

title: Oil Prices CRASH 11-12% — Trump Says War Will End 'Very Soon'
description: Brent crude crashed 11.28% to $87.80/barrel and WTI fell 11.94% to $83.45 after Trump told Republican lawmakers the war could end 'pretty quickly' and 'very soon.' This was the biggest single-day drop since the conflict began. Oil had peaked at $119.50 intraday earlier in the week. Netanyahu simultaneously vowed 'many surprises' for the next phase, revealing a widening gap between US and Israeli war objectives.
factCheckNote: Oil price movements confirmed by CNN Business, CNBC, and Bloomberg; Trump statements confirmed by NPR, Al Jazeera

title: China Bans Rare Earth Exports for Military Use — Global Involvement Deepens
description: China barred export of rare earth elements for military use, a move that could impact US weapons replenishment during the conflict. Russia pledged 'unwavering support' to new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and requested emergency UN Security Council session with China. UK allowed use of Diego Garcia and RAF Fairford for 'specific and limited defensive purpose.' Ukraine's Zelenskyy said a team was heading to the Gulf with drone defense expertise.
factCheckNote: China rare earth ban reported by CNN and TIME; UK involvement confirmed by BBC; Russia-China UNSC request confirmed by Al Jazeera

title: Iran Launches '37th Wave' With Super-Heavy Khoramshahr Missiles
description: Iran launched what it called its '37th wave' of attacks, deploying super-heavy Khoramshahr ballistic missiles — its largest and most powerful — at targets in Israel, US military bases in the Persian Gulf, and Saudi Arabia. The Khoramshahr has a range of 2,000+ km and can carry a 1,800 kg warhead. IDF reported multiple interceptions but missile debris caused casualties.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed Khoramshahr missile type via debris analysis; Al Jazeera and Times of Israel covered the attack

title: 3 More Ships Attacked Near Hormuz — Total 14 Damaged/Sunk Since War Began
description: Three additional commercial vessels were attacked near the Strait of Hormuz on Day 12, bringing the total to 14 ships damaged or sunk since the war began. Shipping traffic through the strait has dropped 95% from pre-war levels. Over 320 vessels now at anchor across the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman. The attacks came despite ongoing US Navy minesweeping operations.
factCheckNote: Lloyd's List and Reuters confirmed the three ship attacks; maritime tracking data shows 95% traffic reduction

title: Lebanon Cabinet Votes 22-2 to Declare Hezbollah Military Activities Illegal
description: In a historic vote, the Lebanese cabinet voted 22-2 to declare Hezbollah's military activities illegal, marking a dramatic shift in Lebanon's relationship with the Iran-backed militia. Hezbollah's political wing remains legal. The move came under intense pressure as Israeli strikes on Lebanon killed over 486 and displaced 700,000. Analysts say this could reshape Lebanon's political future and potentially reduce Iranian influence in the country.
factCheckNote: Lebanese government announced the vote; Reuters and Al Jazeera confirmed the 22-2 tally

narratives.us: Lebanon's vote proves the US strategy is working — Iran's proxy network is collapsing under the weight of this campaign
narratives.iran: The puppet government in Beirut is betraying the resistance under American and Israeli pressure — this vote is illegitimate

title: Trump: 'Practically Nothing Left to Target' — Signals Campaign Near End
description: President Trump stated there is 'practically nothing left to target' in Iran, his strongest signal yet that the US military campaign may be nearing its conclusion. However, Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu continued to push for extended operations, with the US-Israel strategic divergence widening further. The IEA simultaneously approved a record 400 million barrel emergency oil reserve release.
factCheckNote: Trump quote confirmed by CNN and NPR; CENTCOM has not confirmed all targets are exhausted; IEA release confirmed by Reuters and Bloomberg

title: IEA Approves Record 400 Million Barrel Emergency Oil Reserve Release
description: The International Energy Agency approved the release of 400 million barrels from member nations' strategic oil reserves over 60 days — the largest coordinated drawdown in IEA history, surpassing the 2022 release of 182 million barrels during the Russia-Ukraine crisis. The move aimed to stabilize oil markets as the Strait of Hormuz remained effectively closed. Brent crude rebounded 3.4% to $90.77 and WTI rose 2.8% to $86.01.
factCheckNote: IEA official announcement confirmed; Reuters and Bloomberg independently reported; oil price movements verified by market data

title: Hengaw: 4,300+ Killed in Iran — Triple the Government's Official Count
description: The Hengaw Organization for Human Rights, a Kurdish human rights group, reported that over 4,300 people have been killed in Iran since the war began — more than triple the government's official figure of 1,380. With Iran's internet at just 4% of normal capacity, independent verification remains nearly impossible. The discrepancy mirrors past conflicts where Iranian government figures dramatically undercount casualties.
factCheckNote: Hengaw has a track record of reliable reporting on Iranian casualties; figure cannot be independently verified due to internet blackout; BBC Persian reported the discrepancy

title: Spain Permanently Withdraws Ambassador to Israel — Denies US Base Access
description: Spain permanently withdrew its ambassador to Israel, escalating a diplomatic standoff over Madrid's opposition to the Iran war. PM Sanchez condemned the US-Israel attack as 'unjustifiable' and refused to allow the US to use jointly operated military bases in southern Spain for Iran operations. Trump accused Spain of being 'unfriendly' and threatened trade cuts. Both countries are now without ambassadors.
factCheckNote: Spanish official state gazette confirmed the decree terminating Ambassador Sálomon Pérez's appointment; Al Jazeera, JPost, and Times of Israel independently reported

title: FBI Warns California of Iranian Drone Threat Against West Coast
description: The FBI's Joint Terrorism Task Force warned California police departments that Iran 'aspired to conduct a surprise attack using unmanned aerial vehicles from an unidentified vessel off the coast of the United States, specifically against targets in California.' Multiple officials said the threat is not currently deemed credible, but Oscars security was elevated for the March 15 ceremony. Governor Newsom confirmed awareness of the threats.
factCheckNote: FBI alert reviewed by ABC News; multiple US officials told CBS News threat is not deemed credible at this time; Governor Newsom confirmed awareness

title: Iran-Hezbollah Launch Coordinated 'Joint and Integrated Operation' Against Israel
description: Iran and Hezbollah launched a coordinated attack on Israel — five hours of sustained fire striking 50+ targets. Hezbollah fired 150+ rockets at northern Israel while Iran launched ballistic missiles at central Israel simultaneously. Five people injured in Upper Galilee. IDF responded with 'large-scale' strikes on Beirut's Dahiyeh district, hitting 10 Hezbollah headquarters in 30 minutes.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed the coordinated attack; Haaretz, JPost, and Times of Israel reported independently

title: Iran's Ceasefire Demand: US Must Guarantee No Future Attacks — Plus Compensation and Nuclear Rights
description: Iran told regional intermediaries it will only agree to a ceasefire if the US guarantees that neither America nor Israel will strike Iran again. Tehran also demands compensation for war damage and the right to a full nuclear fuel cycle. President Pezeshkian laid out conditions publicly. Israeli Defense Minister Katz responded that operations will continue 'without any time limit.'
factCheckNote: Bloomberg reported based on officials familiar with the matter; Deputy FM Gharibabadi's statements reported by Al Jazeera; Katz response confirmed by JPost

title: Greece Caps Fuel & Food Prices for 3 Months — Europe Scrambles to Contain War's Economic Fallout
description: Greece imposed 3-month profit margin caps on fuel (max 12-cent/liter retail markup) and food to prevent war-driven price gouging. Germany limited gas station price changes to once per day. Italy reviewed using extra VAT revenue to cushion consumers. Brent crude closed at $91.98.
factCheckNote: Greek PM Mitsotakis announcement confirmed by Greek Reporter and Bloomberg; German and Italian measures reported by Bloomberg

title: Six Vessels Attacked in Gulf & Strait of Hormuz — Iraqi Oil Port Operations Shut Down
description: Iranian explosive-laden boats attacked two fuel tankers (Safesea Vishnu and Zefyros) in Iraqi waters, setting them ablaze and killing 1 crew member. Projectiles struck the Thai-flagged Mayuree Naree in the Strait (3 crew missing) and Japan-flagged ONE Majesty near UAE. Total ships struck since war began: at least 16. Iraq shut down oil port operations. US Navy refused merchant escort requests, saying risk is too high.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, AP, and Kathmandu Post all confirm six vessels attacked. Iraqi officials confirmed port shutdown.

title: Iran Threatens US Tech Giant Offices — Publishes Target List of Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia Facilities
description: Iran's Tasnim news agency published a target list of US tech company offices in Israel and Gulf states, labeling them 'enemy technology infrastructure.' Companies named: Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Palantir, IBM, Oracle. IRGC also threatened 'economic centres and banks' of US/Israel after claiming Israel struck an Iranian bank. Employees of international banks began leaving Dubai with families.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel, The Register, and Al Jazeera all reported the Tasnim threat publication independently

title: First Iran Cyberattack on US Company — Pro-Iran Hackers Hit Medical Tech Firm Stryker
description: A pro-Iran hacking group claimed the first significant cyberattack on a US company since the war began, targeting medical technology company Stryker. The group said it was retaliation for the Minab school attack. US intelligence community issued warnings to American companies to harden cyber defenses against Iranian attacks.
factCheckNote: NBC News and ABC News independently confirmed the cyberattack claim and US intelligence warnings

title: Israel Strikes Central Beirut Apartment & Seafront — Lebanon Death Toll at 634
description: Israeli airstrike hit apartment in Aisha Bakkar, central Beirut, near city's biggest mall. AFPTV captured fireball on 7th-8th floors. Separate strike on Ramlet al-Baida seafront killed 7 people. Qana strike killed 5. Updated toll: 634 killed (91 children, 15 healthcare workers), 1,586 injured, 816,000 displaced. UN Security Council meeting called for end to Lebanon fighting.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Cyprus Mail, PBS all reported the strikes; Lebanese health ministry confirmed updated casualties

title: Hezbollah Launches Largest Barrage — 200 Rockets, 20 Drones Hit Northern Israel
description: Hezbollah launched its largest attack since the war began: approximately 200 rockets and 20 drones at northern Israel over several hours. Iran's IRGC called it a 'joint and integrated operation' lasting five hours. Targets included Nahariya, Beit Lid, Glilot (Unit 8200 HQ), Meron, and Atlit naval base. Two lightly injured. Hundreds of thousands sent to shelters. IDF killed IRGC commander Abu Dharr Mohammadi in Beirut retaliatory strike.
factCheckNote: Haaretz, Times of Israel, and Profile News all confirmed the scale and targets of the barrage

title: Iran Fires 'Most Intense' Ballistic Missile Barrage at Tel Aviv and Haifa
description: Iran launched its most intense missile operation of the war, firing advanced ballistic missiles toward Tel Aviv and Haifa. The IRGC described it as part of a joint Iran-Hezbollah operation claiming to have struck over 50 targets. IDF responded with wide-scale strikes across Tehran. Multiple explosions reported in Iran's capital.
factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirm both Iran's barrage and Israel's retaliatory strikes on Tehran

title: Iraq Shuts Oil Port Operations — Tanker Attacks Disrupt Exports
description: Iraq shut down oil port operations after deadly attacks on two foreign oil tankers. The closure further strains global oil supply already hit by the Hormuz blockade. Oil back above $95/bbl. Iran still loading 1.5M bbl/day despite crisis; China receiving 1.25M bbl/day via Iran's oil lifeline.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and multiple outlets confirmed Iraq port shutdown

title: Iran Sets 3 Conditions for Peace — Russia Provides Tactical Drone Advice
description: Iranian President Pezeshkian outlined three peace conditions: recognition of sovereign rights, war reparations, and security guarantees against future aggression. Meanwhile, reports emerged that Russia is providing specific tactical drone advice to Iran, helping hit US and Gulf targets — a new level of military support beyond general assistance. Israel warned Lebanon via US and Western channels: rein in Hezbollah or face infrastructure strikes.
factCheckNote: Iran's peace conditions confirmed by multiple sources; Russian tactical support reported but details unverified

title: Mojtaba Khamenei Issues First Statement — Vows Hormuz Stays Closed
description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first public statement via Telegram, calling for national unity, continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and military resistance. He said Iran is studying 'opening of other fronts.' His physical condition remains uncertain after the Feb 28 strike. The statement contradicts President Pezeshkian's peace overtures, signaling a deepening rift in Iranian leadership.
factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by multiple international outlets including Al Jazeera, Iran International, and Gulf News

title: Netanyahu Holds First Wartime Press Conference — Calls Mojtaba an 'IRGC Puppet'
description: Israeli PM Netanyahu held his first press conference since the war began. He claimed Israel is 'crushing' Iran and Hezbollah, called Mojtaba Khamenei an 'IRGC puppet who cannot show his face in public,' and addressed the Iranian people directly: 'Coming days will bring opportunity to free yourselves, be ready.' He vowed 'many surprises' in the next phase.
factCheckNote: Press conference broadcast live; multiple outlets carried full quotes

title: Brent Crude Surges Past $100 — IEA Releases Record 400M Barrels
description: Oil prices surged past $100/barrel for the first time since 2022, with Brent hitting $100.46 (+9.2%). The IEA agreed to release a record 400 million barrels from emergency reserves, with the US contributing 172 million from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. Stock markets hit 2026 lows with the Dow falling 739 points. Rystad Energy warned Brent could hit $135 if the situation persists 4 months.
factCheckNote: Oil price data from ICE/NYMEX exchanges; IEA release confirmed by member governments

title: Israel Expands Lebanon Operations Past Litani — Strikes Beirut Evacuation Zone
description: Israel expanded military activity in Lebanon with new evacuation orders past the Litani River to the Zahrani River. Overnight strikes hit Beirut's Corniche seafront — a designated evacuation area — killing 8+ people. Hezbollah retook positions south of the Litani that the Lebanese Armed Forces had held under the Nov 2024 ceasefire. UNHCR reported 3.2 million displaced across Iran.
factCheckNote: Evacuation orders confirmed by IDF; strikes confirmed by Lebanese Civil Defense and Al Jazeera on-ground reporters

title: Trump Says War Ending 'Soon' — Israel Says 'No Time Limit'
description: Trump told media the war would end 'soon' because 'there's practically nothing left to target,' adding 'Anytime I want it to end, it will end.' Israeli Defence Minister Katz directly contradicted this, stating the operation 'will continue without any time limit.' Iran's foreign minister Araghchi stated 'We are not asking for ceasefire' while security chief Larijani said 'We will not negotiate with the United States.'
factCheckNote: Trump and Katz statements confirmed by multiple outlets; Araghchi and Larijani quotes from Iranian state media

title: French Soldier Killed in Iraq — First Non-US NATO Casualty
description: French Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion of the 7th Alpine Chasseurs Battalion was killed in a drone strike at a Peshmerga/coalition base near Makhmour in Iraq's Erbil region. Five other French soldiers were wounded. Macron called the attack 'unacceptable.' This marks the first NATO fatality outside the US in the conflict.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by French Ministry of Armed Forces and BNO News

title: Two Oil Tankers Hit in Iraqi Territorial Waters Near Basra
description: Two oil tankers were struck in Iraqi territorial waters near Basra — the first oil-related attack inside Iraqi waters during the conflict. The strikes threaten further disruption to Iraq's southern oil exports and push global crude prices higher.
factCheckNote: Reported by CBS News live updates

title: US KC-135 Stratotanker Crashes in Western Iraq — 5 Crew Fate Unknown
description: A US Boeing KC-135 Stratotanker aerial refueling aircraft crashed near Turaibil in western Iraq, close to the Jordanian border. CENTCOM confirmed 5 crew members were aboard and stated the crash was NOT caused by hostile fire. This is the 4th manned US aircraft lost this month.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM confirmed crash and ruled out hostile fire; CBS and Washington Post corroborate

title: Pezeshkian Sets 3 Conditions for Peace — First Iranian Framework
description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian outlined three conditions to end the war: (1) recognition of Iran's legitimate rights, (2) guarantees against future attacks, (3) compensation and reparations. This is the first formal peace framework from Tehran since the war began.
