factCheckNote: Pezeshkian's conditions reported by Al Jazeera from official Iranian statements

title: Iran Fires 'Most Intense Operation' — Ghadr, Emad, Fattah Missiles at Israel
description: Iran launched what it called its 'most intense operation' of the war, firing Ghadr, Emad, Kheybar Shekan, and Fattah missiles at Tel Aviv, Haifa, Israeli military intelligence HQ, Haifa naval base, radar systems, and US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. Israeli media reported all missiles were intercepted.
factCheckNote: Iran's launch confirmed; Israeli claim of full interception unverified by independent sources

title: WHO Warns of Toxic 'Black Rain' Over Tehran from Oil Depot Strikes
description: The WHO warned that strikes on Iranian oil depots are causing toxic 'black rain' containing benzene and toluene — known carcinogens — to fall over Tehran. The environmental crisis threatens millions of residents and adds a humanitarian dimension to the air campaign against Iranian energy infrastructure.
factCheckNote: WHO warning confirmed by UN News and CBC reporting

title: Brent Crude Closes Above $100 — IEA Announces Largest-Ever Emergency Release
description: Brent crude closed at $100.46/barrel (+9.22%), the first close above $100 since August 2022. WTI hit $95.73 (+9.72%). In response, the IEA announced its largest-ever emergency oil release: 400 million barrels from 32 countries, with the US contributing 172 million from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. Full discharge expected to take ~120 days. Markets remain skeptical the release can offset Hormuz disruption. Dow fell 739 points (-1.56%), S&P 500 -1.52%, Nasdaq -1.78%.
factCheckNote: IEA release confirmed by official IEA press release; oil prices from CNBC market data; stock figures from CNBC

title: NATO Intercepts Third Iranian Missile Over Turkey
description: NATO air defenses intercepted a third Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish airspace near Adana, close to Incirlik Air Base. Residents were woken by air raid sirens at 3:25 AM local time. Turkey condemned the repeated violations of its airspace.
factCheckNote: Turkish government and NATO confirmed the interception

title: KC-135 Crew Deaths Confirmed — All 6 Killed in Iraq Crash
description: CENTCOM confirmed all 6 crew members of the KC-135 Stratotanker that crashed near Turaibil, Iraq were killed. Reports indicate a possible mid-air collision with another KC-135, which landed at Ben Gurion with tail fin damage. Total US military deaths since Feb 28 now stands at 13 service members.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM officially confirmed all 6 crew killed

title: Explosion at Al-Quds Day Rally in Tehran — 1 Killed
description: An explosion rocked an Al-Quds Day solidarity march in Tehran, killing at least 1 person as thousands participated. The cause of the explosion remains under investigation. Al-Quds Day rallies are held annually in support of Palestinians.
factCheckNote: Explosion confirmed by Iranian state media and Al Jazeera reporting

title: Iranian Missile Strikes Zarzir, Northern Israel — 50+ Wounded
description: An Iranian missile struck Zarzir in northern Israel, moderately wounding a woman and injuring over 50 people from shrapnel and flying glass. A teen girl was also killed running to a shelter during the attack.
factCheckNote: Israeli media confirmed strike and casualties

title: Netanyahu's First Press Conference — 'Crushing' Iran
description: Netanyahu held his first press conference since the war began, declaring Israel is 'crushing' Iran and mocking Mojtaba Khamenei for not showing his face publicly. Pentagon chief Hegseth separately claimed Khamenei is 'likely disfigured' and that every Iranian ballistic missile company has been destroyed, with 15,000+ targets struck.
factCheckNote: Press conference confirmed; Hegseth's claims about Khamenei's condition and 15,000 targets not independently verified

title: 11 Iranian Cluster Missiles Penetrate Israeli Defenses — 2 Killed
description: Eleven Iranian cluster missiles penetrated Israeli air defenses, with one dropping approximately 70 cluster bombs over central Israel. Two Israelis were killed. The use of cluster munitions — banned under the 2008 Convention — marks a significant escalation.
factCheckNote: Haaretz investigation confirmed cluster missile penetration and casualty count

title: Michigan Synagogue Attack — Vehicle Rams Temple Israel
description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali rammed a vehicle into Temple Israel synagogue near Detroit, Michigan. The suspect's relatives were reportedly killed in an Israeli strike. The attack represents a domestic spillover of the conflict onto US soil.
factCheckNote: Attack confirmed by law enforcement; suspect identified

title: Treasury Waives Russia Oil Sanctions to Ease Supply Crunch
description: The US Treasury Department waived sanctions on Russian oil exports for one month to free stranded tankers and ease the global supply crunch caused by the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The unprecedented move signals the severity of the energy crisis.
factCheckNote: Treasury waiver confirmed by Time and NBC reporting

title: All 6 KC-135 Crew Members Confirmed Dead in Iraq Crash
description: Pentagon confirms all 6 crew aboard the US Air Force KC-135 tanker that crashed in western Iraq have died. The crash involved a second KC-135 which landed at Ben Gurion with tail fin damage, suggesting a possible mid-air collision. Total US service members killed reaches 13. This is the 4th manned US aircraft lost this month.
factCheckNote: Pentagon and CENTCOM confirmed all crew deaths

title: 2,200 Marines Ordered to Middle East — Ground Operations Not Ruled Out
description: The 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit with 2,200 Marines on USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group (USS Tripoli, USS San Diego, USS New Orleans) plus ~20 F-35Bs ordered from Japan to Middle East. Pentagon refuses to rule out ground operations. Over a week transit time.
factCheckNote: Deployment confirmed by Axios and Military Times reporting from Pentagon sources

title: Highest Volume US Strikes Yet — 15,000+ Targets Hit Total
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth announces the 'highest volume of strikes' over Iran's skies, with over 15,000 enemy targets struck since Feb 28 (1,000+/day). Claims Iran's missile capacity down 90%, drone capacity down 95%. Over 60 Iranian ships and 30 minelayers eliminated.
factCheckNote: Hegseth confirmed at Pentagon press briefing. Independent verification of specific numbers limited.

title: Israel Destroys Zrarieh Bridge — First Civilian Infrastructure Strike in Lebanon
description: Israel destroys the Zrarieh Bridge over the Litani River in Lebanon, the first acknowledged strike on civilian infrastructure. Leaflets dropped over Beirut warn of 'Gaza-scale devastation.' Israeli attacks kill 25+ in Beirut, southern and eastern Lebanon, hitting areas never targeted before. Lebanon toll reaches 773 killed, 1,933 wounded, 800,000+ displaced.
factCheckNote: Bridge destruction confirmed by Al Jazeera and Lebanese sources

title: Brent Crude Closes Above $103 — Second Day Above $100
description: Brent crude closes at $103.14/barrel (+2.67%), second consecutive close above $100. WTI at $98.71 (+3.11%). Despite IEA's record 400M barrel reserve release and US waiving Russian oil sanctions, prices continue climbing. Brent up ~10% for the week.
factCheckNote: Oil prices confirmed by CNBC and commodity exchanges

title: Iran Considers Yuan-Only Hormuz Access — Geopolitical Shift
description: Iran reportedly considering allowing oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if trade is conducted in Chinese yuan, bypassing the US dollar. If implemented, this would represent a major shift in global oil trade dynamics. Iran previously allowed 2 Indian-flagged LPG carriers to pass under Indian Navy escort.
factCheckNote: Reported by Bloomberg; Iran has not officially confirmed the yuan condition

title: US Gas Prices Hit $3.63 — California Above $5/Gallon
description: US national average gas price reaches $3.63/gallon per AAA, up 35 cents in one week and 58 cents from a month ago. California surpasses $5/gallon. Analysts predict $4/gallon nationally by next week. Pre-war average was below $3.00 for 13 consecutive weeks.
factCheckNote: AAA gas price data confirmed

title: Trump Announces Massive Kharg Island Bombing Raid
description: CENTCOM strikes all military targets on Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export hub handling 90% of crude exports. More than 15 explosions reported targeting army defenses, Joshen Sea Base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared. Trump warns he will hit oil facilities next if Iran continues blocking Hormuz.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, CNBC, The Hill, Axios, Washington Post

title: Missile Strikes US Embassy Baghdad Helipad
description: A missile strikes the helipad inside the US Embassy compound in Baghdad. A suicide drone reportedly targeted the embassy's radar system first. The IRGC confirmed this as the '48th phase' of its retaliatory campaign. Smoke seen rising from the compound.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post, AP, Al Jazeera video

title: $10M Bounty on Mojtaba Khamenei and 7 IRGC Commanders
description: State Department announces $10 million reward for information on Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and 7 other senior Iranian officials including IRGC commanders. Largest bounty placed on a sitting head of state since the conflict began.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Israel Hayom, Washington Examiner

title: NATO Intercepts 3rd Iranian Missile Over Turkey
description: Turkey reports NATO defenses intercepted a third Iranian missile that entered Turkish airspace. Residents of Adana near Incirlik airbase woken by sirens at 3:25 AM. Third such incident since the war began, raising tensions between NATO and Iran.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera and Bloomberg

title: 10,000 AI-Powered Merops Interceptor Drones Deployed
description: US Army deploys 10,000 Merops AI-powered interceptor drones to the Middle East. Each costs $14K-$15K, developed by Eric Schmidt-backed Perennial Autonomy and tested in Ukraine. Designed to replace $4M+ Patriot interceptors for counter-drone defense.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg

title: Hegseth: Iran's Ballistic Missile Production 'Eliminated'
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth declares Iran's entire ballistic missile production capacity has been eliminated. Iran's missile volume down 90%, drone volume down 95%. Announces March 14 will see the highest volume of US strikes yet — 'the highest number of sorties and bomber pulses.'
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Time, PBS, HotAir

title: Guterres Arrives in Beirut for Solidarity Visit
description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres arrives in Beirut as Lebanon death toll reaches 773. Calls for de-escalation and dialogue, says the conflict has caused 'immense suffering.' Visit comes as 800,000+ Lebanese are displaced and evacuation orders cover 14% of the country.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News

title: IRGC Threatens UAE Cities — Major Escalation
description: IRGC warns residents of UAE cities to evacuate areas near ports and docks hosting US forces. Calls US positions 'legitimate targets.' First direct threat to a Gulf ally's civilian population. Follows Kharg Island bombing raid.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill and Al Jazeera

title: Iran Fires 3 Ballistic Missiles at Central Israel
description: At least 3 ballistic missiles launched at central Israel. No casualties reported. Sirens sounded across central Israel, smoke seen near Tel Aviv. Iran's missile capability down over 90% per Hegseth — these represent diminishing but continuing threat.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera and Times of Israel

title: Fujairah Port Oil Operations Suspended After Drone Attack
description: Drone attack and resulting fire at Fujairah port (UAE) forces suspension of oil loading operations. Fujairah was the key alternative route to Hormuz — its closure further tightens global oil supply. UAE has absorbed 1,668 strikes since war began (1,422 drones + 246 missiles).
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Gulf News

title: IDF Destroys Iran's Space Research Center and Air Defense Factories
description: Israeli strikes destroy Iran's primary space research center and air defense factories in Tehran. Combined US-Israel total: over 15,000 enemy targets struck, 7,600+ Israeli strikes in Iran and 1,100+ in Lebanon since Feb 28.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 15 coverage

title: CSIS: US War Costs Reach $16.5 Billion Through Day 12
description: Center for Strategic and International Studies updates cost estimate: $16.5B through Day 12, after $3.7B in first 100 hours and $11.3B through Day 6 (Pentagon to Congress). Daily burn rate estimated at $891M-$1.43B. Pentagon requesting $50B supplemental for munitions replenishment.
factCheckNote: CSIS official analysis, Pentagon testimony to Senate confirmed by NBC

title: KC-135 Refueling Plane Crashes in Western Iraq — 6 US Crew Killed
description: A KC-135 Stratotanker from Ohio Air National Guard's 121st Air Refueling Wing crashes in western Iraq. All 6 crew members killed. CENTCOM says cause is not hostile or friendly fire. An Iranian proxy group claims responsibility. Total US military dead now 13.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM confirmed crash and casualties; hostile fire ruled out per official statement

title: 3 F-15E Strike Eagles Downed in Friendly Fire Over Kuwait
description: Three US F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jets shot down over Kuwait in a mistaken friendly fire incident. All 6 crew members ejected safely and were recovered. Pentagon launches investigation into the incident.
factCheckNote: Pentagon confirmed friendly fire incident and safe recovery of all crew

title: Trump Rejects Ceasefire Talks — Claims Iran 'About to Surrender'
description: Trump administration rebuffs multiple ceasefire mediation attempts by Oman and Egypt. Senior White House official says 'He's not interested in that right now.' Trump tells G7 leaders Iran is 'about to surrender' and calls on China, France, Japan, South Korea, UK to send warships to reopen Strait of Hormuz.
factCheckNote: Multiple sources confirm ceasefire rejection and G7 call statements

title: Iran Selectively Reopens Strait of Hormuz for Non-Western Ships
description: IRGC allows passage through Strait of Hormuz for a Turkish cargo ship, 2 Indian LNG carriers, and a Saudi oil tanker. Strait remains 'prohibited' for US, Israeli, and Western-allied vessels. Mojtaba Khamenei vows to maintain blockade indefinitely. 150+ ships remain anchored outside strait.
factCheckNote: Multiple shipping trackers and news sources confirm selective passage

title: Iran Intelligence Ministry Secretly Contacts CIA Through Backchannel
description: Iran's intelligence ministry reaches out to CIA through a third country's intelligence service to discuss potential terms for de-escalation. Ali Larijani and FM Araghchi sought to use Oman as conduit for talks involving VP JD Vance — efforts have not yet materialized into formal negotiations.
factCheckNote: Reported by NYT and Jerusalem Post citing intelligence sources

title: Israel Plans Major Ground Invasion of Southern Lebanon
description: Israel planning massive ground operation south of the Litani River to dismantle Hezbollah infrastructure. Triggered by Hezbollah's 200+ missile coordinated barrage with Iran. Lebanese government outlaws Hezbollah military activities and announces it will arrest anyone connected to IRGC.
factCheckNote: Reported by Axios citing Israeli military officials

title: IEA Authorizes Record 400M Barrel Oil Reserve Release
description: International Energy Agency authorizes largest-ever coordinated oil reserve release of 400 million barrels. US contributing 172M barrels from Strategic Petroleum Reserve. Despite release, Brent crude remains above $100/barrel at $103.14. WTI at $98.71.
factCheckNote: IEA official announcement confirmed; prices verified via commodities markets

title: NPR/PBS Poll: 56% of Americans Oppose Iran War
description: NPR/PBS/Marist poll shows 56% of Americans oppose the Iran war. Market stress approaching 'Liberation Day' tariff shock levels. S&P 500 down 3% in March, now in red for year. Goldman Sachs pushes rate cut forecast from June to September.
factCheckNote: NPR/PBS/Marist polling methodology confirmed

title: US Strikes 90 Military Targets on Kharg Island — Oil Spared
description: CENTCOM strikes 90 military targets on Kharg Island — Iran's critical oil export hub handling 90% of crude exports. Targets include naval mine storage, missile bunkers, air defenses, naval base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar. Trump deliberately spares oil infrastructure but warns of 'immediate reconsideration' if Iran blocks Hormuz.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Washington Post, Al Jazeera, NPR, Time

title: Iran Retaliates — Missiles Hit Israel, Eilat, Gulf States
description: Iran launches retaliatory missiles targeting central Israel, Eilat (child moderately injured, 2 lightly hurt), and Jerusalem. Iran claims Khaibar missiles struck Netanyahu's office — no impact confirmed in Jerusalem. Qatar intercepts 4 ballistic missiles. Kuwait's Ahmad Al-Jaber Air Base hit by drones. Fujairah (UAE) fire after drone debris.
factCheckNote: Missile launches and Eilat casualties confirmed. Iran's claim of hitting Netanyahu's office unconfirmed.

title: Iran-Linked Hackers Hit Stryker — 60+ Hacktivist Groups Active
description: Iran-linked hacking group 'Handala' launches major cyberattack on medical devices company Stryker. Employee phones wiped worldwide, computer access blocked. CISA investigation launched. Pro-Russian group NoName057(16) teams with Iranian hacktivists to target Elbit Systems. Poland reports possible Iranian hack on nuclear research center. ~60 hacktivist groups active.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, Al Jazeera, Palo Alto Unit 42, PBS

title: Hegseth: Iran Military 'Destroyed' — $10M Reward for Khamenei Info
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth claims Iran's military is 'destroyed.' Missile capability reduced 90%, drone capability down 95%. Claims new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei is 'wounded and likely disfigured.' State Dept offers $10M reward for Khamenei's location. US-Israeli strikes have hit 15,000+ targets across 200+ cities.
factCheckNote: $10M reward confirmed by State Dept. Capability reduction figures not independently verified. Khamenei condition unconfirmed.

title: UNHCR: 3.2M Iranians Displaced — Humanitarian Crisis Deepens
description: UNHCR reports 3.2 million Iranians internally displaced. 100,000 fled Tehran in first 72 hours. 56 cultural sites suffered serious structural damage. 29 clinical facilities damaged, 10 forced to close. 2.5M Afghan refugees in Iran also at risk. UNESCO urges protection of heritage sites. Lebanon: 830,000 displaced, 826 killed (Health Ministry).
factCheckNote: UNHCR displacement figures confirmed. Lebanon Health Ministry figures verified.

title: Heavy Overnight Strikes — Isfahan, Shiraz, Tehran, Dezful, Hamedan Hit
description: US-Israeli forces conduct heavy overnight bombing across Iran. 20 explosions reported near Shiraz, heavy strikes on Isfahan area, southern Tehran, Dezful Air Base, Khomein, and Hamedan. Part of the continued campaign that has now struck over 6,000 targets across Iran.

title: IRGC Launches 10 Hypersonic Missiles at Al Dhafra Air Base in UAE
description: Iran's IRGC claims it launched 10 missiles and several drones targeting US forces stationed at Al Dhafra Air Base in the United Arab Emirates. The IRGC also warned it would target any vessel linked to the US, Israel, or their allies transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

title: IRGC Vows to 'Pursue and Kill' Netanyahu
description: Iran's IRGC issues direct threat against Israeli PM Netanyahu. 'IRGC vows to pursue and kill child-killer Netanyahu if he is still alive,' Iran's IRNA news agency reports. The statement comes amid ongoing speculation about Netanyahu's wellbeing after he failed to appear publicly.

title: Iran Claims Drone Strikes on Israeli Police HQ and Gilat Satellite Center
description: Iran's army claims special police unit headquarters and the Gilat defence satellite communications centre were hit by drone attacks. Israeli officials have not confirmed these specific claims.

title: Iran FM Araghchi Sets Conditions for War's End
description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the end of the war depends on guarantees that attacks will not be repeated and payment of compensation for damage caused during the conflict. This marks the most specific set of conditions Iran has laid out for ending hostilities.

title: Trump: 'Not Ready for a Deal — Terms Aren't Good Enough'
description: President Trump tells NBC News that Iran 'wants to make a deal' but he is not ready because 'the terms aren't good enough yet.' He also calls on allies who rely on Gulf oil to send warships to help keep the Strait of Hormuz open, claiming 'many countries' would assist including China, France, Japan, South Korea, and the UK.
factCheckNote: Iran has set conditions (guarantees + compensation), not an unconditional desire to 'make a deal' as characterized. China and UK have not confirmed sending warships.

title: Casualty Update — Iran: 1,444 Killed, 18,551 Injured
description: Iran's Health Ministry reports 1,444 killed and 18,551 injured since strikes began on February 28. The Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA) estimates the true death toll may be higher at 1,858+, including both civilians and military personnel. In Lebanon, 826 have been killed including 106 children since March 2.

title: Lebanon Overnight Strikes Kill 4 — Hezbollah Intel Chief Eliminated
description: Israeli overnight strikes in southern Lebanon kill at least 4 people. The IDF announces that head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters, Hussein Makled, was killed in overnight strikes in Beirut. Axios reports Israel is planning a major ground invasion south of the Litani River.

title: Oil Closes Above $100 for Second Day — Gold Hits $5,032
description: Brent crude closes at $103.14/bbl (+29.2% from pre-war). WTI at $98.71. Gold reaches $5,032/oz. The IEA's release of 400 million barrels from strategic reserves has failed to stem prices as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. US gas averages $3.68/gallon, up 15.7% from pre-war.
